common.requestTimeout: The request timed out — Joulenap didn't respond in time. Check it is running and try again.
common.backendUnreachable: Can't reach Joulenap — showing last known data.
common.notConfigured: Joulenap isn't set up yet — connect your Proxmox VE and PBS to start backing up.

unsaved.title: Discard unsaved changes?
unsaved.body: You have edits here that haven't been saved. Leaving now discards them.

auth.sessionExpired: Your session expired. Please sign in again.
auth.signInSubtitle: Enter your credentials to manage the scheduler.
auth.registerSubtitle: No account configured. Create the administration credentials to get started.
auth.timezoneHint: Detected from your browser. The backup schedule runs in this timezone — you can change it later under Settings.

errors.userShort: The username must be at least 3 characters.
errors.passShort: The password must be at least 8 characters.

dashboard.retentionHelp: Counts are combined, not added. Proxmox keeps the newest backup in each period, and a backup can count for several — so 3 daily + 2 weekly + 1 monthly is not 6. Each period counts from today: "weekly 2" = this week + last week; "monthly 1" = only this month's latest. To keep a backup from a month ago, set monthly to 2.
dashboard.wakeRetriesHelp: If the PBS doesn't come up within the wait timeout, re-send the Wake-on-LAN packet this many more times before giving up. 0 = try once.
dashboard.scheduleCustom: Custom schedule set in config.yaml ({{cron}}) — edit it there to change.
dashboard.actionFailed: Couldn't start the action. Check the PBS and try again.
dashboard.guestsError: Couldn't refresh the guest list — showing the last known guests.
dashboard.noGuests: No guests found on this node.
dashboard.noGuestsSelected: Select at least one guest, or switch to General backup — Selective mode with no guests backs up nothing.
dashboard.excludeNote: Exclude mode is set in config.yaml — every guest is backed up except the ones marked ✕. Edit config.yaml to change the list.
dashboard.runsError: Couldn't load the run history — retrying.
dashboard.noTaskLog: No task output yet — appears here live during backup, GC and verify.

confirm.backupMsg: An immediate backup will start, outside the current schedule. If the PBS is asleep it will be woken first (up to {{timeout}}s).
confirm.gcMsg: Garbage collection will start. If the PBS is asleep it will be woken first (up to {{timeout}}s); it can temporarily reduce datastore performance while running.
confirm.keepOn: Keep PBS powered on after the job
confirm.stopMsg: The {{job}} in progress will be told to stop, and the task running on the server is cancelled with it. Data already written is kept; the run is recorded as aborted.
confirm.stopPowerOff: Power the PBS off after stopping
confirm.onMsg: A Wake-on-LAN packet will be sent to the server. Startup can take up to {{timeout}} seconds.
confirm.offMsg: The PBS will be shut down now. The scheduler will wake it automatically for the next scheduled backup.

localization.title: Localization
localization.subtitle: Set the interface language and the timezone the scheduler runs in.
localization.timezoneHint: Timezone the backup schedule is interpreted in. Leave on Automatic to use the TZ environment variable, falling back to UTC.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Send an alert when a backup finishes. Delivered via Apprise — fill in a channel below or add any Apprise URL.
notifications.onSuccessDesc: Send a message when a backup completes successfully.
notifications.onFailureDesc: Send a message when a backup fails or is aborted.
notifications.customDesc: One URL per line — any Apprise service or plain webhook.
notifications.saveFirst: Save your changes before sending a test.

safety.title: Backup safety
safety.subtitle: Guardrails around the backup cycle: refuse to back up onto a nearly-full datastore, avoid interrupting a busy PBS at power-off, and keep trying to wake the PBS if it's slow to boot.
safety.freeDesc: Abort the backup if the PBS datastore has less than this much space free, so a backup never starts onto a nearly-full store.
safety.guardTitle: Wait for running tasks before power-off
safety.guardDesc: Before powering the PBS down, wait for any task it is running (a manual GC, verify or sync) to finish, so a clean shutdown never interrupts it. If it's still busy after the wait, the PBS is left on.
safety.verifyWarnTitle: Verification re-reads your backups — it can take a long time.
safety.verifyWarnBody: Verifying re-reads stored snapshots and re-checks every chunk's checksum to catch silent corruption. It runs entirely on the PBS (your guests aren't touched), but it reads the actual backup data — anywhere from a few minutes to several hours depending on how much data you've backed up and how fast the datastore disk is. It also keeps the PBS awake longer, using more energy. Both options below are off by default.
safety.afterDesc: Right after each backup, verify just the new snapshots while the PBS is still awake. Already-verified data is skipped, so this stays quick.
safety.schedDesc: On a schedule, wake the PBS on its own and verify the whole datastore, then power it back off. This is the slow, thorough check.
safety.reverifyHint: Skip snapshots verified more recently than this, so each run mostly checks new data. 0 = re-verify everything every time (slowest).

setup.title: Guided setup
setup.subtitle: Each step enables the next: detected fields fill in automatically — you just confirm.
setup.completeBanner: Joulenap is set up. Your PVE/PBS connection is saved — you can leave this as is, edit a step, or reset to run the wizard again.

reset.confirmMsg: This clears the saved PVE/PBS connection so you can run the wizard from scratch. Your backup schedule, notifications and login account are kept. The tokens stay valid in Proxmox until you revoke them there.

trust.title: Your root password is never stored
trust.body: Prefer no root at all? Use token setup and paste your own scoped tokens. If you pick quick setup, the root password is used once — in memory — to create least-privilege API tokens and a poweroff-only SSH key, then discarded. Only the scoped tokens are written to config.yaml, and you can revoke them anytime in Proxmox.
trust.source: Open source (AGPL) — read exactly what runs during setup in connectors/provision.py & sshkey.py.

why.pve: Joulenap connects to Proxmox VE to list guests and start backups.
why.node: Choose the node to back up and the PBS storage backups are sent to.
why.pbs: Joulenap reads datastore status and runs GC/verify on the PBS.
why.wol: Lets Joulenap wake the powered-off PBS with a Wake-on-LAN packet.
why.ssh: PBS has no shutdown API, so Joulenap powers it off over SSH.

note.pveRoot: Root password used once, in memory, to mint a scoped token — then discarded. Only the token is saved.
note.pveToken: Paste a scoped API token — no root password ever reaches Joulenap.
note.pbsRoot: PBS root used once to mint a token scoped to this datastore, then discarded.
note.pbsToken: Paste a PBS token (datastore read + GC) — no root needed.
note.ssh: The installed key is locked to a single poweroff command — no shell, no forwarding — and the private key never leaves this box.

does.pve: Creates a least-privilege role and API token, then binds them:
does.pbs: Creates a token scoped to this datastore (GC + status) and node status:
does.ssh: Installs exactly this one line in the PBS root's authorized_keys:
does.tighten: Want it even tighter? Create a custom role by hand and paste a token in token setup — see docs/CONFIG-WIZARD.md.
does.manualHint: Prefer to run it yourself? Execute these on the host, then use token setup and paste the result.

ssh.hostKeyWhy: Confirm this matches your PBS's SSH host key before the password is sent.

hints.wolIface: Interface Joulenap sends the Wake-on-LAN packet from; once the PBS host is set the packet is scoped to its subnet, not the whole network.
hints.pbsRoot: Used once to create the PBS API token and install the shutdown SSH key, then discarded.
hints.sshManual: Add the line below to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys on the PBS, then mark as installed.
hints.fingerprintPinned: This fingerprint is pinned — connections are rejected if it changes.

errors.pbsUnreachable: PBS is not reachable at that host and port. Check it is powered on and the address is correct, then try again.
errors.pbsTokenMissing: A PBS API token is required. Enter the token ID and secret before continuing.
errors.macNotDetected: Couldn't auto-detect the MAC address — enter it manually.

integrations.title: Dashboard integration
integrations.subtitle: Expose backup status to a homelab dashboard (Homepage, Homarr, Dashy, Glance) via a read-only API endpoint.
integrations.keyShownOnce: Copy this key now — it won't be shown again.
integrations.copyFailed: Couldn't copy automatically — select the text and copy it manually.
integrations.snippetHint: Paste this under a group in your dashboard's config and keep the indentation intact.
integrations.keyHiddenNote: A key is configured. Regenerating replaces it — the current key stops working immediately.
integrations.regenerateConfirmBody: The current key will stop working immediately. Any dashboard using it must be updated with the new key.
integrations.disableConfirmBody: The endpoint will return 403 and any dashboard widget using it will stop working.

updates.title: Update check
updates.subtitle: Ask GitHub once a day whether a newer Joulenap release exists, and show a badge in the footer if so.
updates.toggleHint: Off by default: with this disabled Joulenap never contacts the internet.
updates.upToDate: Up to date — v{{version}} is the latest release.
updates.unknown: Couldn't reach GitHub — will retry later.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.subtitle: Settings with no home on the other screens. If you are not sure what one does, leave it alone.
advanced.modeHint: Snapshot backs up a running guest live. Suspend and Stop pause or shut down each guest for the duration of its backup — more consistent, but the guest is unavailable.
advanced.bwlimitHint: Caps the vzdump write rate. 0 means unlimited.
advanced.retentionHint: Daily, weekly and monthly are on the dashboard's Schedule card. These two are the remaining vzdump buckets.
advanced.keepLastHint: Always keep this many most-recent backups regardless of age. 0 disables the bucket.
advanced.keepYearlyHint: Number of yearly backups to keep. 0 disables the bucket.
advanced.historyDaysHint: Run history and activity-log rows older than this are pruned daily so the database can't grow without bound. 0 keeps everything forever.
advanced.restartHint: ⚠️ These three only take effect after the container is restarted.
advanced.portHint: Port the app listens on inside the container. Changing it also means changing the port mapping of your container.
advanced.sessionDaysHint: How long a sign-in stays valid before the login screen comes back.
advanced.httpsOnlyHint: Enable when Joulenap is served over HTTPS or behind a TLS-terminating proxy. Leaving it on over plain HTTP will lock you out of signing in.
advanced.yamlSubtitle: The whole configuration, exactly as it is stored. Everything the forms don't cover lives here.
advanced.yamlHint: Secrets are shown as ***REDACTED*** — leave them as-is to keep the stored value, or type a new one to replace it. A key you delete keeps its current value; the document is validated before anything is written.
advanced.copyFailed: Couldn't copy automatically — select the text and copy it manually.
